meta.description: Curated frontier AI/ML signals scored and classified against the current learning phase. act_now = reinforce now, monitor = file for later, parked = defer, ignore = hype.

signals.title: Essence of Linear Algebra — Chapter 7: Dot Products and Duality
signals.summary: Grant Sanderson's visual explanation of dot products using duality — why dot product equals the length of a vector projection times the other vector. Core Week 4 content, visualized perfectly for commute listening.
signals.reason: Direct Week 4 content reinforcement. Dot products are core linear algebra. 3Blue1Brown visual approach makes abstract math concrete — ideal active recall support.
signals.action: Watch tonight or during morning commute. After: implement dot product in NumPy and verify the projection interpretation.

signals.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero — micrograd walkthrough (backpropagation from scratch)
signals.summary: Karpathy implements a scalar autograd engine in 100 lines of Python. Teaches backpropagation conceptually before frameworks. Month 3 content but watching now builds the mental model that makes all future math coherent.
signals.reason: Backprop is Month 3 content but this video is the foundational conceptual bridge. Watching passively now while focusing on Week 4 linear algebra is the right move — it will make Month 3 immediate rather than abstract.
signals.action: Add to Later queue. Watch in full during Month 2 capstone week. Passive first-pass during gym commute is OK.

signals.title: Ollama now supports tool calling — local function calling without cloud APIs
signals.summary: Ollama 0.2 ships tool/function calling support for compatible models (Mistral, Llama 3). You can now build local agents that call Python functions with no API key. Week 6 local LLM content now includes tool use.
signals.reason: Week 6 is local LLMs. Tool calling in Ollama is a direct extension of the Week 6 install-and-run exercise. High relevance_score because it is implementation-ready and commuter-friendly read.
signals.action: Read during lunch. After reading: update Ollama, test tool calling with a simple Python function wrapper.

signals.title: Claude API now returns token-level streaming for tool use blocks
signals.summary: Anthropic updated the streaming API to include incremental tool_use deltas. This means you can stream function call arguments character-by-character, enabling smoother UX for agent applications.
signals.reason: REST API patterns are Week 3. Streaming LLM APIs are Month 2 content. Understanding this now gives you a preview of where the Week 3 API patterns lead. File for active implementation in Month 2.
signals.action: Skim now. Add link to LATER.md under Month 2 API section. Full implementation during Month 2 Claude API wrapper project.

signals.title: Visual intuition for eigenvectors — community explanation thread with animations
signals.summary: Top-voted community explanation thread. Multiple animated GIFs showing eigenvector stretching/rotation. Best crowd-sourced explanation of why eigenvectors matter for PCA and covariance matrices.
signals.reason: Eigenvalues/eigenvectors are a confirmed weak topic. This is direct reinforcement material. Community threads give different framings that 3B1B videos do not.
signals.action: Read during lunch (requires screen for animations). Make a one-sentence definition of eigenvectors in your own words after reading.

signals.title: Understanding Sentence Embeddings — from word2vec to modern embedding models
signals.summary: Raschka's biweekly newsletter deep-dive on how sentence embeddings encode semantic meaning. Covers word2vec → GloVe → sentence-transformers pipeline. Excellent bridge between Month 1 linear algebra and Month 2 embeddings.
signals.reason: Embeddings are Month 2. But the article explicitly shows how dot products and cosine similarity (Week 4 content) become the basis for semantic search. Filing for Month 2 but skimming intro now makes Week 4 math feel purposeful.
signals.action: Add to LATER.md under Month 2 Embeddings. Skim intro paragraph only this week — read in full Month 2.

signals.title: LLaMA 4 Scout: 17B MoE with 10M context window — local inference implications
signals.summary: Meta releases LLaMA 4 Scout (17B mixture-of-experts, 10M token context). Runs on a single A100 or M3 Max with Ollama quantization. Opens up very long-context local inference for the first time on consumer hardware.
signals.reason: Local LLMs are Week 6. LLaMA 4 Scout may be available via Ollama by the time you reach Week 6. File for reference — check Ollama model library on Week 6 start day.
signals.action: Skim HF model card. File. On Week 6 start: run `ollama list` to check if Scout is available.

signals.title: Gemini 2.0 Flash: Multimodal reasoning at 1M token context
signals.summary: DeepMind technical report on Gemini 2.0 Flash architecture. Sparse mixture-of-experts with cross-modal attention. Scores SOTA on MMLU, HumanEval, and math benchmarks.
signals.reason: Architecture details require transformer foundations (Month 4+). Reading this now would be analogous to reading an advanced recipe before learning to cook. File for Month 4.
signals.action: Park. Add to LATER.md under Month 4 Transformers. Do not read until after implementing attention from scratch.

signals.title: Building a full agentic RAG pipeline in 50 lines of LangChain
signals.summary: Community post walking through a RAG + agent + tool calling pipeline using LangChain. Full code, Docker setup, ChromaDB integration.
signals.reason: Agents require embeddings (Month 2), RAG (Month 3-4), and LLM API mastery (Month 2-3). This is the destination, not where you are. Save it for the capstone phase — it will read completely differently after you have the foundations.
signals.action: Park in LATER.md under Month 4 Agents. Bookmark for reference during capstone.

signals.title: AGI is 6 months away — leaked internal Google memo claims 99% human-level reasoning
signals.summary: Viral Reddit post claiming a leaked Google memo predicts AGI by Q3 2026. No verifiable source. Third-party speculation about internal benchmarks.
signals.reason: No engineering value. Unverified speculation. Zero implementation path. This is exactly what the 70/20/10 discipline is designed to filter out.
signals.action: Discard. Do not engage. Protect your focus budget.
